title: MFRC522 RFID Card Reader (NTAG216)
design_question: Which RFID standard provides adequate security for an office access-control system?
selected_approach: MFRC522 reader + NTAG216 NFC tags (ISO 14443A, 7-byte UID). SPI at 10 MHz. UID + timestamp HMAC-SHA256 signed in firmware.
rationale: NTAG216 has a unique 7-byte UID not easily cloned; HMAC prevents replay attacks. Mifare Classic CRYPTO1 is broken since 2008.

alternatives.reason_rejected: CRYPTO1 cipher broken; UID cloneable with £5 device—rejected on security grounds.
GDPR complexity for biometric data; higher cost—rejected.

verification_notes: FACT: 'RFID/NFC Reader — ISO 14443 / 15693 Interface' explicitly warns about Mifare Classic CRYPTO1 weakness and recommends NTAG216.
